track_listing.track: What Makes The World Go Round
'Ain't No Love In The Heart Of The City'

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. TV on the radio here. Thomas the Vance. And welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
And this evening we broadcast two of the great groups who helped to make Reading a massive success this year.
Samson from London and those Wolverhampton wild men Slade who re-emerged from the smaller clubs onto the big stage and knocked everybody out.
And we've got classic cuts from Jethro Tull, Fleetwood Mac, Whitesnake, The Pink Floyd, among others, and new stuff by Vardis and by Moore and by Taurus.
Those two latter tracks coming from Metal Inc. Explosion, an album that's coming out next week, which features sessions from the Friday Rock Show.
We've also got the Friday Night Connection, but this evening we start with this.
Looking forward to seeing that band on Monday night when they perform in London.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
That's Kiss and their new single, which is called What Makes the World Go Round.
Generally music and love, I suggest.
And before that you heard something from Highway to Hell by ACDC
The track was called Walk All Over You
And that was for Jane and for Chris and for Matt and for Mark and for Tim and for Nicky
And also for Jane and for Chris
I hope you've got good results in your O-Levels and A-Levels
With lots of love from Steph who lives in Chilham in Kent
And also for everybody who attends, I think it's the Malham Public School
Particularly James and Andrew and Warren
And for Gary and Nick and Rick and Steve and Paul C
and everybody from 4E and 4C of Ellis-Gulseford School, I think it is.
That's a comprehensive school.
With love from Julie, the headbangerette Mason.
And also for all the good friends of Adam Kinnersley from Cheshire.
And for somebody I spoke to on the phone today,
Martin Heider and Brother Chris and all his mates.
And now, here's something quite old.
[Music]

hoping to see you on the 6th, can't get there because
of course i'm going to be working on rock on saturday but i hope the weather's good for all of
you who congregate around the albert memorial in high park tomorrow for the genesis picnic
and living in the past was also for jillian thomas jill thomas let's not be formal she lives in

monster selling album rumors the track was called dreams and that was for the sister whose name i
don't have of a gentleman by the name of phil sister of phil you live in barnsley and a happy

they receive good wishes from pauline and miriam alias the principal and yeti from south shields
And I've got a nice letter here from Mrs. Anne Yeoman
of 50 Privet House, Cumberland Street, Portsmouth, in hands,
who wanted me to play a record by Whitesnake,
which happened to be Ain't No Love in the Heart of the City,
for her daughter, Tracy,
who lives, I think it is, in Gatley, near Manchester.
And also for husband, Roger,
it's the anniversary of your marriage on September 23rd.
And not forgetting Mark, who lives down there in Pompey.
You're listening to the Friday Rock Show from Radio 1.
It's twenty-eight minutes before eleven.
And now we're going to broadcast a little storming scent here
that was recorded at Reading by a band called Samson.
[Music]
I love a Samson, recorded up there at the Reading Festival a couple of weeks ago.
And the tracks you heard were Hammerhead, Walking Out On You, Vice Versa, Hard Times, and Take It Like A Man.
Four-piece band, as if you didn't know.
Paul Samson, Bruce Bruce, Chris Aylmer, and Thunderstick, the masked man behind the drums.
and incidentally they're going to be at least two of them are
that's Thunderstick and Bruce Boos on Rock on Saturday tomorrow afternoon
the mix was by Robbo the Maid of Ale Madman
and production was by Tony Wilson
and it's three minutes now to eleven
on the Friday Rock Show from BBC Radio 1
from the BBC with the matrix number REH397
on BBC Records
coming out next week is going to be an album called Metal Explosion
featuring Samson, Praying Mantis, Trespass, Taurus
Angel, Witch, Gillen, Money and More.
And all the tracks on the album have been broadcast on this programme
and were recorded specially for the programme.
In other words, they were sessions.
And I'm going to play you a couple of cuts now.
The first one's by More and the second one is by Taurus.
This one's called Soldier.
A couple of tracks that are going to be released on an album,
[Music]
which comes out next week.
You heard there Taurus and Paper Chaser.
Before that, it was More and Soldier.
And the album comes out next week on BBC Records and Tapes.
And it's called Metal Explosion
9 minutes after 11 and it's time for the Friday Night Connection
And also a long-winded explanation which I'll try to pricey and keep relatively short
Because we like to play a lot of music on this program
Right, the tracks we played you last week were Gypsy Eyes by Jimi Hendrix
From the LP The Essential Jimi Hendrix
That's the LP we played it all
Souped Up 4 by Rory Gallagher from the LP Against the Grain
And Planet Caliban by Black Sabbath from Paranoid
now the answer as set was that all the guitarists were left-handed now we
actually believe that to be incorrect so what we've done in our infinite wisdom
fools that we are we've scrapped the whole of that connection and we didn't
get too many entries this week obviously so not too many people have lost 12p
stamps and we're sorry basically that's what I'm trying to say in other words
will you accept please that last week's connection was null and void because we
don't believe that the answer was absolutely correct. We could, really, to be honest, have
potted it up and given you a load of different explanations and found another connection,
but we thought rather than do that, we'd tell you the truth. That really is what we're all
about. So what I've done this week, if you'll excuse me, is to put together a Friday night
connection myself. Do I get the £10 record token? Of course I don't. For making the
mistake, I've got to give them £10. Wilson is standing over me now, about to collect
it this connection was put together by tommy vance it's not that difficult but you'll have
to think about it it's in there somewhere but that's my friday night connection said especially
[Music]
for you and really it was an excuse just to play three of the greatest tracks that i know
three tracks that i just keep on playing at home again and again and again but there is a definite
connection in it and if you think you know what it is jot it down on a postcard and send it to
the friday night connection the friday rock show radio one london w1a4 ww and if you get it all
right and we'll send you a 10 pound record token don't forget to put together your own friday night
connection as well sorry about the muck up last week but better to tell you the truth he thinks
okay now let's return to our recorded live from reading set and i think one of the biggest
surprises was slayed when they hit the stage now they're a band who've been on the small circuit
for a long time haven't been on a big stage for some considerable years really when they went on
the stage they were received warmly as you will hear right here and that for me was one of the
magic moments in rock and roll that i will never ever forget and i've been in rock and roll on and
off for quite a number of years and that was just a magic moment the re-emergence of slade to a big
big stage and the tracks that you heard were come on feel the noise and also merry christmas but
then the crowd sang that get down and get with it mama we're all crazy now walk alone sung again by
the Reading crowd. Something else
medley, with all various bits and pieces
that they put in there. The Wheels Ain't
Coming Down, that's a new song of theirs. When I'm
Dancing, which is also a new song that I've not heard
before, and then Take Me Back Home.
And as I say, it was just
a magic moment. The
right band on stage at the right time,
and an audience that were so
receptive. I think the lads are on the way
to Belgium at the moment. Slade, thank you very much
for that set. I think you're down in Dover at the
moment. Have a good time. It was
